sections.paragraph:
II. ἄπειμι (s. εἶμι) impf. ἀπῄειν (Hom.+) to move from a position, go away, go, come, go away ἀπό τινος fr. someth. (SIG 1218, 19). εἰς τὰ ὄρη AcPl Ha 5, 8. Simply go, come (Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, Jac. εἰς Χανάαν; Jos., Ant. 14, 289) εἰς τ. συναγωγήν (cp. 4 Macc 4:8) Ac 17:10. εἰς τὴν δωδεκάφυλον GJs 1:3 (some mss., not pap). ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ 10:2; 16:3. πρὸς τὴν συγγενίδα 12:2; πρὸς τὸν ἱερέαν 15:2. ἄ. οὗ ἐὰν βούλησθε wherever you wish 1 Cl 54:2. ἀπῄει μετʼ αὐτοῦ (the midwife) went with him (Joseph) GJs 19:1. Of the day depart (cp. Ins. 10, 1: Eranos 13, 1913, p. 87) 1 Cl 24:3.—DELG s.v. εἶμι.

impf. impf. = imperfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

someth. someth. = something

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Eranos Eranos , Acta Philologica Suecana—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 100
